cases.name: mv comparison: moves a file and removes the source
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; echo hi > $D/src; mv $D/src $D/dst; cat $D/dst; [ -f $D/src ] && echo bad || echo ok; rm -rf $D

cases.name: mv comparison: moves a file into an existing directory
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; mkdir $D/dir; echo hi > $D/src; mv $D/src $D/dir; cat $D/dir/src; rm -rf $D

cases.name: mv comparison: moves a directory into an existing directory
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; mkdir $D/srcdir; mkdir $D/dstdir; echo hi > $D/srcdir/file; mv $D/srcdir $D/dstdir; cat $D/dstdir/srcdir/file; rm -rf $D

cases.name: mv comparison: mv to the same path is a no-op
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; echo hi > $D/file; mv $D/file $D/file 2>/dev/null; cat $D/file; rm -rf $D

cases.name: mv comparison: overwrites an existing destination file
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; echo old > $D/dest; echo new > $D/src; mv $D/src $D/dest; cat $D/dest; rm -rf $D

cases.name: mv comparison: moves a symlink without dereferencing it
cases.script: D=/tmp/jb_mv_$$; rm -rf $D; mkdir $D; cd $D; echo hi > target; ln -s target link; mv link moved; readlink moved; cat moved; rm -rf $D
